projectDescription: A self-hosted, open-source platform for running social-media misinformation experiments — build a simulated feed, randomise between-subjects conditions, collect participant data, and export analysis-ready results.
keyFeatures: Visual study builder with Configurator tab — screens, parts, post layout, questions, all in one place
A/B manipulation — between-subjects split with per-post content variants (Headline A vs B)
Metric conditions (social proof) — second between-subjects factor controlling visible like/share counts
Conditional logic — deterministic if→then rules to skip parts, screen out participants, or end the study early
Multi-part experiment design — sequence of feed or pager parts with transition screens and interaction requirements
Dashboard — configurable widget grid (KPIs, charts, crosstabs) with public shareable read-only link
Multi-sheet Excel export plus CSV — raw reactions, session summaries, credibility ratings, 2×2 design breakdown
In-app analyses — t-test, ANOVA, chi², correlation, regression, Cronbach's alpha
Panel recruitment integration — capture external respondent IDs, completion and screen-out redirect URLs
Eye tracking — optional webcam gaze tracking via WebGazer.js, exported to a dedicated sheet
Multi-user accounts — admin and researcher roles, invite-only, per-study ownership and access grants
AI-assisted translation — optional Anthropic API key powers automatic study-content translation
MS Clarity integration — per-study project ID, demographic masking, virtual URLs for per-screen heatmaps
Preview mode — fully functional study walkthrough that stays out of the real dataset
